items.title: Polymarket traders cut Clarity Act passage odds to record low as Senate delay drags on
items.description: Polymarket bettors have cut the odds of the CLARITY Act passing this year to a record low as Senate negotiations over ethics provisions remain unresolved.

items.title: Stripe and Swift race to control the next generation of global payments infrastructure
items.description: Crypto and blockchain experts say this week's moves show the two established finance companies are increasingly competing for control of the infrastructure behind digital payments.

items.title: Cardano hands core development to outside teams in decentralization push
items.description: Input Output will transfer control of the Haskell node, Plutus, Hydra and other components as founder Charles Hoskinson says the network must change and start growing again.

items.title: Inside Robinhood’s high-stakes bet to onboard millions of casual users onto decentralized finance
items.description: The company says its advantage lies in bringing retail customers onchain, but early activity remains dominated by memecoins, while its original tokenization vision remains small.

items.title: Japan's SBI Group is building Asia's first cross-border digital asset empire
items.description: The securities giant announced today the consolidation of Singapore-based Coinhako as part of a rapid regional expansion that includes a tokenization partnership with Ondo Finance.

items.title: Bitcoin faces fresh headwinds as China’s Kimi beats Claude, GPT in coding benchmark
items.description: Moonshot's Kimi K3 took the top spot in frontend coding away from Claude and OpenAI, and it's free. Semiconductor stocks fell, and crypto fell with them.

items.title: AI frenzy losing steam leaves bitcoin less volatile than South Korean stocks
items.description: Your day-ahead look for July 17, 2026

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin returns to $63,000 as Nasdaq trims large early loss
items.description: A deepening global selloff in chipmakers dragged risk assets lower, pulling bitcoin back from the $65,000 level it reached on this week's soft inflation print.

items.title: FTX to distribute $900M to creditors in fifth payment round
items.description: The FTX Recovery Trust and company have distributed about $10 billion since the exchange filed for bankruptcy in November 2022, leaving users cut off from their funds.

items.title: Galaxy lands 15-year Texas Tech stadium naming rights deal
items.description: Galaxy Digital will rename Texas Tech’s football stadium under a 15-year agreement, expanding its West Texas presence as the state attracts growing crypto investment.

items.title: Consensys unknowingly outsourced developer work to North Korean
items.description: Through an introduction with a “reputable third-party service provider,“ the company took on a developer who, as part of an investigation, was revealed to be tied to North Korea.

items.title: Crypto Biz: When dollars disappear, stablecoins step in
items.description: Bolivia moves to recognize USDT amid a dollar shortage, while Bitcoin miners’ AI ambitions face fresh investor scrutiny.

items.title: OKX Europe lets users convert USDT to MiCA-compliant USDC
items.description: The feature offers European customers a voluntary path away from Tether’s USDT as MiCA rules reshape the region’s stablecoin market.

items.title: Senator Warren requests 2026 reporting for Trump’s crypto earnings after $1.4B disclosure
items.description: With the Senate likely voting on a crypto bill within days, Elizabeth Warren asked for information on Donald Trump’s earnings between January and July ahead of a 2027 deadline.

items.title: Bitcoin price sags under $62.5K as Iran strikes add to US stocks pressure
items.description: Bitcoin saw a key rejection at local highs before reversing lower, moving with stocks for a second day as US-Iran war downside took its toll.

items.title: HSBC wins Bank of England approval to enter Digital Securities Sandbox
items.description: The Bank of England approved HSBC Orion to go live in its Digital Securities Sandbox, with the first Digital Gilt Instrument transaction expected in the first quarter of 2027.

items.title: Trump rips Canada as wildfire smoke spreads, says U.S. will add pollution cost to tariffs
items.description: The smoke coming from active wildfires in Canada has led to unhealthy air quality in swaths of the U.S., which could affect the World Cup final in New Jersey.

items.title: Inside the Chinese fraud rings stealing billions from banks and retailers
items.description: Chinese organized crime groups are making as much as $1 billion annually in tap-to-pay fraud schemes targeting retailers and banks.

items.title: The White House is dictating access to frontier AI models, shifting power from tech giants, sources say
items.description: The Trump administration is taking steps to control who gets access to the latest frontier models, people familiar with the matter told CNBC.

items.title: Taco Bell says it has removed lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak from its restaurants
items.description: Taco Bell, which the CDC linked to the cyclosporiasis outbreak, will likely recover soon from the health safety scare, according to analysts.

items.title: Economic outlook is worsening and Trump is getting blamed, CNBC survey finds
items.description: The public is as depressed about the economy as it has been since the years just after the pandemic, according to the All-America Economic Survey.

items.title: Leather jacket worn by Nvidia CEO goes for just under $1 million at Sotheby's auction
items.description: The high price for the garment is a sign that collectors are looking to bid on artifacts and collectibles from the artificial intelligence boom.

items.title: Chinese AI has leveled up, and brought renewed focus on the open weight model shift
items.description: It's the latest AI model from China to close the performance gap with leading U.S. AI labs.

items.title: Epstein victims blast Trump attorney general nominee Todd Blanche after meeting
items.description: "Todd Blanche treated the meeting as a mere 'check-the-box' exercise intended to secure votes for his confirmation," said Epstein survivor Dani Bensky.

items.description: British overseas territory is expanding into the sea to relieve cramped living conditions

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hyodIJXUPHA! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Meet Kimi K3, the newest Chinese AI model haunting Silicon Valley
items.description: Moonshot AI’s new open model is catching up to Anthropic and OpenAI, raising questions about the future of the AI race.

items.title: The World Cup–winning side will make $50 million — and the IRS gets a cut
items.description: “It doesn’t make a difference who wins the game. The IRS will get a piece.”

items.title: SpaceX postponed a crucial launch — and its stock went on to close below IPO price for second day
items.description: Shares in SpaceX declined about 4% in premarket trading on Friday after aborted mission.

items.title: The average stock is having a moment as semiconductors struggle. It’s a sign of a healthy market.
items.description: It has been a good week to be average on Wall Street.

items.title: 19 (mostly) tech stocks that have fallen at least 25% in July
items.description: Seven of the worst performers for the month were still up by triple digits for 2026.

items.title: How your winning stock-index fund can turn on a dime
items.description: Also in Weekend Reads: The changing bond market, housing-market opportunities and advice from the Moneyist.

items.title: Look on the bright side: Energy companies are booming
items.description: You can’t beat them. Maybe you should join them.

items.title: Chip stocks have entered a bear market. A BofA analyst says not to panic.
items.description: The semiconductor sector is undergoing a reset and has a tendency to underperform in the third quarter.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Britain’s system for avoiding electricity blackouts faces scrutiny
items.description: National Energy System Operator’s task made more difficult by climate change and increasing use of renewables

items.title: The London IPO market isn’t as bad as it looks
items.description: It is easy to look enviously at New York, but the UK is on course to enjoy its best year for new listings since 2021

items.title: Why your odyssey to see ‘The Odyssey’ matters
items.description: The extremes that film fanatics are going to in order to watch Nolan’s latest tell us something

items.title: Asia AI bets power record equities run for Wall Street banks
items.description: Investments in semiconductors fuel growth in regional revenues for US banks

items.title: Superdrug owner considers delay to planned London IPO
items.description: Hong Kong-based AS Watson was aiming for a dual listing this autumn

items.title: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa al-Thani, former ruler of Qatar, 1952-2026
items.description: The ‘Father Emir’ transformed the Gulf nation into a financial and geopolitical heavyweight

items.title: Gibraltar land grab stirs age-old dispute with Spain
items.description: British overseas territory is expanding into the sea to relieve cramped living conditions

items.title: How long until there is a US markets reckoning over Trump’s damage?
items.description: The patience of the bond markets will ultimately wear thin

items.title: Cyclospora Investigation Turns to Farms in Mexico
items.description: The F.D.A. is planning to inspect farms and a lettuce shredding facility to determine how lettuce supplied by the produce company Taylor Farms to Taco Bell became contaminated and where it was shipped.

items.title: Taylor Farms to Remove Products After Lettuce Is Linked to Cyclospora Cases
items.description: The California company grows produce in more than 12 states, and in Mexico and Canada. Taco Bell and food distributor Sysco have pulled Taylor Farms lettuce from supply chains.

items.title: China’s Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi Model, Threatening America’s Lead
items.description: China’s Moonshot AI unveiled a freely available artificial intelligence model that seemed to narrow the gap with cutting-edge offerings from U.S. tech companies.

items.title: X and Music Publishers Retreat From Lawsuits
items.description: Elon Musk’s social media platform and the music companies agreed to dismiss dueling claims, from 2023 and 2026. They have not said why.

items.title: After Repeated Crises, Boeing Looks to Turn a Corner
items.description: The company recently opened a new production line to keep up with strong demand for its 737 Max, though orders for its new planes lag rival Airbus.

items.title: Pentagon’s Escort Policy for Journalists Is Temporarily Restored
items.description: An appeals court said the Defense Department could require escorts for reporters who visit the Pentagon while The New York Times sues to overturn the rule.

items.title: America’s Enterprising Spirit Is Booming After Decades-Long Slump
items.description: Hundreds of thousands of people are creating companies each year, ending a decades-long drought.

items.title: Roger Summit, Who Invented an Early Online Search Service, Dies at 95
items.description: A research scientist at Lockheed, he came up with the idea for a computer system that would search scientific and technical literature almost instantaneously.
